Issue 1 RCA: MItem.barcodes (@ElementCollection → item_barcode_list) had a = new HashSet<>() field initializer. fromPojo() via ModelMapper always left it as a plain non-PersistentSet empty HashSet because the Item POJO has no barcodes field. On em.merge(), Hibernate treated this as "collection explicitly cleared" and issued DELETE FROM item_barcode_list. @PreUpdate then ran collectBarCodes with barcodeList=null, cleared the set, and added nothing — wiping all rows silently.
Issue 1 CAPA:
TC Added - GM-306328 
1. TC: Update item price only → verify barcodes unchanged in DB.
2. TC: Server restart → non-barcode save → barcode count unchanged.
3. TC: PPS scan after non-barcode MDM update → should not crash.
Issue 2 Title: Tote not getting picked from relay point due to multiple tote_storage reservations | APOTEK
Issue 2 RCA: when a ranger_process crashes multiple time and the task has to be manually deassigned from the ranger, in a normal crash-restart cycle this is handled by recomputing subtask and compute_tote_store calls unreserve_storage_location at the start of the next cycle to clear leftovers. However, if the supervisor path calls deassign task flow (e.g. after repeated crashes exceeding the restart threshold), the task is deassigned with no restart queued and no cleanup called. The reservations are then permanently orphaned, no future compute_tote_store for that tote ever runs to clear them.
Issue 2 CAPA:
1. The 3 test cases tickets have been created to test tote_storage reservation clear in regression SUITE 
https://greyorange-work.atlassian.net/browse/GM-308466

Issue 5 Title: Apotek RTP | Butler Manager Crashing On a Specific Coordinate
Issue 5 RCA: Usually when a bot carries a rack, and if it gets deassigned, rack is lifted down and another bot comes to pick it up and store it in a valid storable location (this is called rack-restore task). To store the rack, we search for empty location, if this config allow_deepest_store_multi_direction is true, then we search in all directions and store the rack in deepest storable. If the config is false, we check for empty location and search deep in that direction only. But here in this case, the deassignment happened at a valid storable location only, but all the surroundings were not empty so it didn't get any direction and also allow_deepest_store_multi_direction was false, so the direction passed was undefined which caused the crash.
Issue 5 CAPA: Every new and existing config introduced in a code should be covered with all the scenarios in CTs and same should be tested by QA with all the states.
Issue 6 Title: Apotek || Tpid resolution not working correctly
Issue 6 RCA: Whenever server restarts, item_order_mapper was recreated but it gets empty. Any order which was created before the restart will lose the data in this cache and the tpid_resolver use this cache to get the order ids corresponding to changed tpids. Hence those orders were not getting resolved.

Issue 11 Title: Apotek || PPS stuck due to Item barcode missing on mdm
Issue 11 RCA: MItem.barcodes had a field initializer = new HashSet<>(). When a non-barcode save went through ModelMapper.fromPojo(Item), it produced an MItem with barcodes = new HashSet<>() and barcodeList = null. On entityManager.merge(), Hibernate treats a plain (non-PersistentSet) empty set as "explicitly cleared" and issues DELETE FROM item_barcode_list. The @PreUpdate callback then ran collectBarCodes with null barcodeList, cleared the set, and added nothing — confirming the wipe.
Issue 11 CAPA: Shared with GM-306288. Add regression to release checklist.

Learning 1 Note: GM-306288 | MItem.barcodes (@ElementCollection → item_barcode_list) had a = new HashSet<>() field initializer. fromPojo() via ModelMapper always left it as a plain non-PersistentSet empty HashSet because the Item POJO has no barcodes field. On em.merge(), Hibernate treated this as "collection explicitly cleared" and issued DELETE FROM item_barcode_list. @PreUpdate then ran collectBarCodes with barcodeList=null, cleared the set, and added nothing — wiping all rows silently.
Learning 2 Note: Add the leaked bugs into test cases and also put in the final test checklist
Learning 3 Note: Requirement gaps should be captured durinf
Learning 4 Note: task-deassign supervisor paths must call cleanup routines unconditionally — not just on normal crash-restart cycles.
Learning 5 Note: CT To be included for the following scenarios:All task-deassign supervisor paths must call cleanup routines unconditionally — not just on normal crash-restart cycles. Hot code patches changing gen_server record definitions must include code_change/3 callbacks — enforce via PR checklist. Never use eager field initializers on @ElementCollection fields — Hibernate treats a plain empty set on merge as an explicit delete. Every FSM must have exhaustive rehydration clauses for all state × rack_status combos; unhandled states cause crashes or stuck seats. In-memory caches must be repopulated on server restart so downstream resolvers don't fail on pre-restart data. Every PUT/PATCH API needs regression tests confirming that updating one field does not clear or overwrite unrelated fields.
Learning 6 Note: Test Case to be included following scenarios: Crash-and-recovery test cases are mandatory: simulate crashes beyond restart thresholds and verify no orphaned reservations or subtasks. Any deploy touching config maps requires a pre-deployment throughput regression run in staging; a drop vs baseline must block release. Every new or modified config must be covered by CTs across all valid states; CT coverage gates QA sign-off. Bugs caught in nightly regression on develop must be confirmed backported to the release branch before the next release window.
Learning 7 Note: Test to included for following scenarios in Checklist:When a PUT API supports partial updates, explicitly define which fields are optional vs mandatory — ambiguity causes silent data loss at ORM layer. Sim and prod configs must be kept in parity and diffed before every release; config drift is a direct source of throughput regressions. Deployment runbooks for config-sensitive releases must include a config-diff review step and a ≥2h staging validation run. Edge-case order states (hybrid tpid, stuck tags, audit-cleared orders) must be explicitly mapped in solution design — undefined handling causes silent stuck orders. Designs relying on Kafka event ordering must specify how FSM/PPS states handle out-of-order or lagged events without deadlocking.
Learning 8 Note: Edge cases and scenarios to be included in the FSD and ISD
